# Drive Ulcinj Like a Local: Compare Car Rentals from €20/day

Ulcinj car rental is the fastest way to reach **Velika Plaža, Ada Bojana, Valdanos Cove, Šasko Lake, and the Old Town Ulcinj area** on a schedule that matches your trip, not a bus timetable. Local agencies such as **Prego Rent a Car, MCommerce, Elezovic Rent, Rent a Car 999, MontenegroCar, Tim Rent, Kalamper Petrol, and MTL Rent a Car** offer economy cars from **€20/day**, while airport pickups at **Podgorica Airport TGD** and **Tivat Airport TIV** can add convenience if you are crossing the **Sozina Tunnel** or driving the **Adriatic Highway / Jadranska Magistrala** toward the **Adriatic coast** and the **Montenegrin Riviera**. For many travelers, the best fit is a small manual like a **Hyundai i10, Hyundai i20, Opel Corsa, Peugeot 208, Škoda Fabia, or Citroën C3**, because local pricing is usually lower than platform averages and a compact car is easier on narrow streets in the old center.

> 💡 **Tip:** If your route includes **Velika Plaža, Ada Bojana, or the Ulcinj Riviera**, book a compact economy car early and confirm hotel delivery or airport pickup in writing. Local operators in Ulcinj often quote **€20–€35/day** for manual economy cars and **€45–€70/day** for automatics or SUVs, so a written confirmation helps avoid pickup-day price changes.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** Do not assume every rental includes the same protection level; ask for the exact terms for **CDW, SCDW, FDW, excess/deductible, franchise, zero excess, full coverage, third-party liability, and TPL**, because a low headline rate can still leave you paying a **preauthorisation** and a large deductible at pickup.

## Why You Need a Car in Ulcinj

Ulcinj is compact in the center, but the destinations that define the trip are spread across the **Montenegrin Riviera**, from **Mala Plaža** and the **Old Town Ulcinj** promenade to **Valdanos Cove**, **Ada Bojana**, **Velika Plaža**, and the inland link toward **Virpazar** and **Skadar Lake**. Montenegro recorded **2.6 million tourist arrivals in 2024**, and the country’s transport network still leaves many beach and nature spots poorly served by scheduled buses, so a car gives you a measurable time advantage for day trips.

A rental car also helps on longer coastal drives along the **E-65 / E-851 / R-17 corridor** and the **Adriatic Highway (Jadranska Magistrala)**, especially if you want to reach **Cetinje**, **Stari Bar**, **Sveti Stefan**, or the **Bojana River** area without waiting for a taxi. For a route plan that pairs beaches with inland stops, see our [beaches and day trips](/beaches-day-trips) guide.

> 💡 **Tip:** Plan at least one full day for the **Ulcinj Riviera** and a second day for the **Bay of Kotor** or **Skadar Lake** if your itinerary includes both coast and inland sightseeing. A realistic loop from Ulcinj to **Virpazar** and back can take 3–5 hours without long stops, depending on traffic and border checks.

## What Does Car Rental Cost in Ulcinj?

Ulcinj rental prices depend on season, transmission, pickup point, and insurance package, and the most useful price signal is the total daily cost including **CDW** or **SCDW** rather than the base rate alone. In direct quotes from local agencies, a **Prego Rent a Car** economy model can start at **€20/day**, while **Elezovic Rent** and **Tim Rent** often quote **€45/day or more** for compact cars; on comparison sites, peak-season rates commonly rise into the **€62–€67/day** band.

For evidence-based budgeting, use the table below and compare the vehicle class, not just the brand name:

**Ulcinj Car Rental Price Comparison**

| Season | Economy Manual | Compact Manual | SUV / Automatic |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Peak (Jul-Aug) | €40–€55/day | €55–€70/day | €70–€95+/day |
| Shoulder (Jun, Sep) | €30–€45/day | €45–€60/day | €60–€80/day |
| Off-season (Oct-May) | €20–€35/day | €35–€50/day | €45–€65/day |

Insurance affects the final bill as much as vehicle class: **CDW** commonly adds **€10–€20/day**, **SCDW** or **full coverage** can add **€25–€50/day**, and a security **excess/deductible** may range from **€200** for an economy car to **€5,000** for premium vehicles. Some agencies call this a **franchise**, and many require a **preauthorisation** on a credit card before release.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** A cheap daily rate can still become expensive if the agency blocks a large deposit, so check the exact amount for the **preauthorisation**, the stated **franchise**, and whether the quote includes **zero excess** or only partial coverage.

### Top Local Rental Companies in Ulcinj

Each company below has a different pricing profile, fleet size, or service model, and the best choice depends on whether you need airport delivery, an automatic, or the lowest possible daily rate.

**Prego Rent a Car:** Prego Rent a Car is a local value option with economy cars from **€20/day**, a **4.4/5 Google rating from 12 reviews**, and delivery across Montenegro, including hotel handoff and pickup near the main bus station in Ulcinj.

**MCommerce:** MCommerce is a WhatsApp-first operator with phone number **+382 67 653 674**, hotel delivery, and a Booking.com partner listing, which makes it useful for travelers who want direct messaging and a paper trail.

**Elezovic Rent:** Elezovic Rent is a small cash-oriented agency with a **three-car fleet**, **CDW included**, free additional drivers, baby seats, and a Montenegro-only driving policy.

**Rent a Car 999:** Rent a Car 999 is an automatic-only operator with unlimited mileage, **24/7 support**, over **15 Montenegro locations**, and free cancellation up to **48 hours** before pickup.

**MontenegroCar:** MontenegroCar is a larger multi-language operator with an extensive fleet, one-way rentals, hotel delivery, and mixed Trustpilot feedback, so it suits travelers who want breadth of choice more than the lowest headline rate.

**Tim Rent:** Tim Rent is a smaller local provider often used for compact manuals such as the **Peugeot 2008**, **Renault Megane**, or **VW Passat**, and direct booking can be competitive when you need a larger sedan for coastal driving.

**Kalamper Petrol:** Kalamper Petrol is useful for airport or roadside pickup along the **Adriatic Highway**, and travelers often use it as a practical fuel-and-rental stop when driving toward **Ulcinj** from **Bar** or **Sveti Stefan**.

**MTL Rent a Car:** MTL Rent a Car is another Montenegro operator worth checking for short-term local use, especially if you need a simple economy car such as a **Hyundai i10**, **Hyundai i20**, or **Peugeot 208**.

## Essential Tips Before You Book

For summer travel, book **5–7 days** ahead, while shoulder-season travel often works with **2–3 days** of lead time; this matters because Ulcinj demand rises quickly around weekends and school holidays, especially on routes that start at **Podgorica Airport TGD** or **Tivat Airport TIV**. If your route crosses the **Sozina Tunnel**, the **Adriatic Highway (Jadranska Magistrala)**, or the **E-65 / E-851** corridor, confirm your pickup and drop-off times in writing so the agency cannot argue about late arrival or out-of-hours fees.

Use a **full-to-full** fuel policy whenever possible, and keep the last fuel receipt because fuel disputes are common at return desks; as of **February 2026**, gasoline is about **€1.64/L** and diesel about **€1.33/L**, so a refill can be priced accurately if you return with the tank full. If you want to drive from Ulcinj to **Shkodër** via the **Sukobin/Muriqan border**, ask for a **Green Card** and written permission for Albania before pickup, because cross-border travel without approval can void coverage.

> 💡 **Tip:** Ask the agency to list the exact insurance wording on the voucher: **third-party liability (TPL)**, **CDW**, **SCDW**, and whether the policy is **zero excess** or still has a deductible. A written voucher is stronger than a verbal promise at the counter.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** Do not rely on “theft insurance” alone as a substitute for full protection, because the agency may still require a **preauthorisation** and may exclude damage caused by driver error, underbody contact, or border crossings outside the approved countries.

## Driving Routes and Local Destinations from Ulcinj

The most useful Ulcinj rental routes connect the coast, lakes, and border crossings in a single day, and a compact car is often enough for the paved roads leading to **Velika Plaža, Ada Bojana, Valdanos Cove, Mala Plaža, Old Town Ulcinj, Stari Bar, Sveti Stefan, Virpazar, Cetinje, Skadar Lake, and the Rumija mountain foothills**. If you are heading inland, the road toward **Cetinje** and **Skadar Lake** is easier to manage in a manual economy car than in a large SUV, especially when parking near markets or old stone streets.

For border and nature routes, the paved corridor to **Shkodër** and the **Sukobin/Muriqan border** is the most common southern-day-trip option, while the drive to **Bojana River** and **Ada Bojana** is the fastest beach escape from town. If you need a wider regional overview, our [driving and parking guide](/driving-parking-guide) explains local road signs, paid parking, and toll-related planning.

> 💡 **Tip:** If you plan to visit **Korzo promenade**, **Old Town Ulcinj**, or **Mala Plaža** at peak hours, park outside the tightest historic streets and walk in; a small car like a **Citroën C3** or **Škoda Fabia** is easier to maneuver than a full-size sedan.

## FAQ

### Can I rent a car in Ulcinj without a credit card?
Yes, some Ulcinj agencies allow cash or debit-card rentals, but the exact policy depends on the company and insurance package. For example, **Elezovic Rent** is described as cash-only, while larger operators such as **MontenegroCar** or airport desks often prefer a card for the deposit, which may be a **preauthorisation** rather than a charge. The exception is that many agencies still require a valid card for the security block even when the final payment is made in cash.

### Is cross-border travel from Ulcinj to Albania allowed?
Yes, cross-border travel to Albania is possible if the agency authorizes it and issues the right paperwork. Many rentals can provide a **Green Card** for the **Sukobin/Muriqan border**, and the drive from Ulcinj to **Shkodër** is one of the most practical international day trips from the city. The caveat is that some local fleets restrict travel to Montenegro only, so always verify the border rule before pickup.

### Is full coverage worth it for car rental in Ulcinj?
Yes, full coverage can be worth it if you want predictable costs and lower out-of-pocket exposure. Local quotes commonly price **CDW** at **€10–€20/day** and **full coverage/SCDW** at **€25–€50/day**, which can be cheaper than paying a large **excess/deductible** after a claim. The exception is that if your trip is short and you are comfortable with a higher deductible, a basic policy may be more cost-effective.

### What is the cheapest time to book a rental in Ulcinj?
The cheapest time is usually the off-season, especially **December**, when some deals fall to about **€13/day** and the broader **October–May** period often stays in the **€20–€35/day** range for economy cars. Rates are lower because demand is thinner outside the summer beach season and airport arrivals at **Podgorica Airport TGD** are less concentrated. The caveat is that fleet choice is smaller in winter, so automatic transmissions and larger SUVs may sell out first.

> 💡 **Tip:** If you need a guaranteed automatic, compare **Rent a Car 999**, **MontenegroCar**, and airport pickup desks first, because automatic stock is limited during July and August.

# Ulcinj Airport Car Rental: Complete Guide to Podgorica Airport (TGD) and Tivat Airport (TIV)

Planning an **Ulcinj airport car rental** starts with one decision that affects time, tolls, and summer traffic: **Podgorica Airport TGD** is usually the faster arrival point, while **Tivat Airport TIV** can be practical only outside peak season or for routes that continue along the **Adriatic coast**. For most Ulcinj trips, a rental car is the most flexible option because it lets you reach **Old Town Ulcinj**, **Velika Plaža**, **Ada Bojana**, and nearby stops such as **Stari Bar** without relying on limited bus timetables. If you want a route-focused breakdown, see our [Driving & Parking Guide for Ulcinj](/driving-parking-guide).

> ⚠️ **Warning:** July and August traffic on the **Adriatic Highway**, including the **Bay of Kotor**–Budva corridor and the **Jadranska Magistrala** sections toward the south, can add 1.5–2.5 hours to a normal drive.  
> In 2025, traveler reports and local road checks consistently showed major congestion on the coastal corridor, while inland routes via **E-65** and **E-851** stayed more predictable.  
> If your flight lands in summer, build a buffer into your pickup plan or choose **Podgorica Airport TGD**.

## Podgorica Airport TGD or Tivat Airport TIV for Ulcinj?

For an Ulcinj arrival, **Podgorica Airport TGD** is the better all-round choice because the route is shorter, less exposed to coastal congestion, and easier to price accurately with a rental car, taxi, or prebooked transfer. **Tivat Airport TIV** is acceptable if you are combining Ulcinj with the **Montenegrin Riviera**, **Bay of Kotor**, or a stop near **Sveti Stefan**, but the summer traffic risk is materially higher.

| Airport | Distance to Ulcinj | Typical drive time | Road/toll notes | Best for |
|---|---:|---:|---|---|
| **Podgorica Airport TGD** | 79 km | 1 hr 20 min to 1 hr 30 min | **Sozina Tunnel** toll €2.50; inland exit via **E-65** and **R-17** can avoid some congestion | Fastest Ulcinj transfer |
| **Tivat Airport TIV** | 85 km | 1 hr 45 min to 2 hr 30 min off-season; 3 hr to 4.5 hr in July-August | Scenic coast via **Adriatic Highway / Jadranska Magistrala**; no tunnel toll on the standard coastal run | Coastal road trips |

The practical difference is not just kilometers: the **Sozina Tunnel** route from Podgorica typically saves 20–60 minutes compared with the Tivat coastal approach, and that advantage becomes larger when holiday traffic builds around Budva and the **Bay of Kotor**. In a 2025 travel-season comparison used by local operators, the Podgorica corridor remained the more reliable option for arrivals before 16:00.

**Direct recommendation:** choose **Podgorica Airport TGD** if you want the lowest-risk Ulcinj arrival, and choose **Tivat Airport TIV** only if your flight price, connection quality, or onward itinerary along the coast is clearly better. For car pickup logistics, compare local suppliers such as **Prego Rent a Car**, **MCommerce**, **Elezovic Rent**, **Rent a Car 999**, **MontenegroCar**, **Tim Rent**, **Kalamper Petrol**, and **MTL Rent a Car**.

> 💡 **Tip:** If you land at **Podgorica Airport TGD**, ask your rental desk or delivery agent to confirm the fastest route to Ulcinj before departure.  
> A local driver may choose the **Sozina Tunnel** route for speed or the inland **Virpazar** road for a toll-free option.  
> This matters most on Fridays and Sundays, when southbound traffic near **Bar** and **Virpazar** can slow the standard route.

## Best Route From Podgorica Airport to Ulcinj

The default GPS route from **Podgorica Airport TGD** to Ulcinj usually uses the **Sozina Tunnel**, which is the quickest option in normal conditions and the most predictable route for a rental car. The tunnel is 4,185 meters long, the toll is €2.50, and payment is made in euros at the booth; travelers should expect occasional slowdowns near the approach road during summer and holiday peaks. Montenegro’s road network has limited tolls, and the Sozina crossing is the main cost on this route.

A toll-free alternative uses **Virpazar** and the **Skadar Lake** area, then continues toward **Bar** and the south coast. This option is useful if you want to avoid the tunnel fee, stop in **Cetinje** on a longer road trip, or add a scenic detour through vineyards and lake viewpoints. On standard conditions, the inland route usually adds 15–25 minutes versus the tunnel route.

**Road note:** the highway alignment between Podgorica and the coast is mainly associated with **E-65**, while southern links toward Ulcinj and the border region are commonly referenced as **E-851** and **R-17** on local route planners. In practice, signage can vary, so a GPS app with offline maps is helpful.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** The **Sozina Tunnel** is the fastest route, but it is not the cheapest if you are counting every transfer cost.  
> The toll is €2.50 per crossing, and the tunnel can slow during peak holiday hours when southbound rental traffic meets heavy bus flow.  
> If you are arriving with children, luggage, or a late-night flight, confirm whether your rental contract includes roadside assistance and **third-party liability (TPL)** before you depart.

## How Much Does an Airport Transfer to Ulcinj Cost?

The cost of getting from **Podgorica Airport TGD** or **Tivat Airport TIV** to Ulcinj depends on whether you book a rental car, use a taxi, or connect by bus and train. For most travelers, a rental car is the best value because the price is transparent and the car can be used for the full holiday, including beach runs to **Velika Plaža** and **Ada Bojana**.

| Option | Typical price | Best value for | Caveat |
|---|---:|---|---|
| **Local rental car** | From about €22/day for economy cars | Multi-day Ulcinj stays | Deposit and insurance terms vary |
| **Regulated taxi from Podgorica** | About €90 for a sedan, €120 for executive class, €160 for an 8-seat minivan | One-way airport transfers | Price usually includes tolls, but always confirm |
| **Bus connection via Bar** | About €4–€5 for Ulcinj to Bar, plus onward fares | Budget travelers | Requires a transfer and fixed schedules |
| **Train via Skadar Lake** | About €2 from Bar to Virpazar | Scenic side trips | Not a direct airport-to-Ulcinj transfer |

Local transfer prices are influenced by season, vehicle size, and pickup time. In summer 2025, airport taxis were often fully booked at late arrival banks, while local rental firms still offered prearranged meet-and-greet pickup if the reservation was confirmed in advance. If you need route ideas after pickup, our [beaches and day trips](/beaches-day-trips) page covers the **Ulcinj Riviera**, **Valdanos Cove**, **Mala Plaža**, and the drive toward **Shkodër**.

## Airport Pickup or Hotel Delivery in Ulcinj?

For most Ulcinj visitors, the main decision is not just which airport to use; it is whether the car should be collected at the terminal or delivered to a hotel in town. **Airport pickup** is best when you want immediate departure after landing, while **hotel delivery** is best when you want the agent to meet you after check-in in **Old Town Ulcinj** or near the promenade. Local companies often accept more flexible payment methods than international brands, including cash and card, but every rental should still be checked for **preauthorisation**, deposit rules, and driver age conditions.

**Prego Rent a Car:** This local supplier is known for airport delivery to **Podgorica Airport TGD** and **Tivat Airport TIV**, plus handover in Ulcinj and other coastal towns; a booking note should confirm the exact pickup hour and the final fuel policy.

**MCommerce:** This operator is commonly used for meet-and-greet service at arrivals, and its practical advantage is flexible delivery for arrivals that land after the main office hours.

**Elezovic Rent:** This local company is useful for travelers who want a smaller fleet and direct communication with the agent, especially for late-season pickups.

**Rent a Car 999:** This company is frequently mentioned for multi-location returns and airport pickup, and a reservation can be paired with hotel delivery in Ulcinj if arranged before arrival.

**MontenegroCar:** This supplier often appeals to travelers who want a broader vehicle choice and a straightforward pickup process at the airport or in town.

**Tim Rent:** This company is a practical choice for short stays and local road trips because it typically focuses on simple handover rather than complex add-ons.

**Kalamper Petrol:** This name appears often in local mobility searches, and the practical value is proximity to road corridors used by travelers heading toward the coast.

**MTL Rent a Car:** This firm is relevant for airport and town deliveries, especially when a traveler wants a compact car and a direct handover without a long queue.

**AMSCG:** This road-assistance and motoring entity is a useful reference point for current road conditions, safety notices, and local driving guidance in Montenegro.

For Ulcinj-specific beach driving, ask for a car that is easy to park in town and comfortable on short coastal hops. A **Hyundai i10**, **Škoda Fabia**, **Citroën C3**, or **Opel Corsa** is usually sufficient for city use, while a **Hyundai i20**, **Peugeot 208**, or **Renault Megane** gives a bit more luggage space. If you plan longer routes to **Cetinje**, **Skadar Lake**, or the **Sukobin/Muriqan border**, consider a **Peugeot 2008** or **VW Passat** for better comfort on mixed roads.

> 💡 **Tip:** Ask for **full coverage** if you plan to drive daily between Ulcinj, **Ada Bojana**, and the beach parking zones near **Velika Plaža**.  
> Many local offers combine **CDW** plus **SCDW** or a **zero excess** package, while others show a normal **franchise** or **excess/deductible** of €300–€1,000.  
> The exact deposit can change by driver age, season, and vehicle class, so request the written voucher before paying the **preauthorisation**.

## Rental Insurance in Montenegro: CDW, SCDW, and Excess Explained

Car rental insurance in Montenegro is usually sold in layers, and the key difference is how much financial responsibility remains if the vehicle is damaged or stolen. A standard rental often includes **third-party liability (TPL)** by law, while collision protection is typically sold as **CDW**; a stronger package may be labeled **SCDW**, **FDW**, **zero excess**, or **full coverage** depending on the supplier. The important detail is always the remaining **excess/deductible** or **franchise**, which is the amount you may still owe after a claim.

| Insurance term | What it usually means | Typical financial effect |
|---|---|---|
| **TPL / third-party liability** | Covers damage or injury to other people or property | Usually mandatory; does not cover your rental car |
| **CDW** | Collision Damage Waiver | Reduces your liability but often leaves an excess |
| **SCDW** | Super Collision Damage Waiver | Lowers the excess substantially |
| **FDW** | Full Damage Waiver | Often marketed as near-complete damage coverage |
| **Zero excess / full coverage** | The driver pays little or nothing at claim time | May exclude wheels, glass, tires, or underbody damage |

For a rental in Ulcinj, the best practice is to verify the insurance wording before you leave the airport because some companies advertise “full coverage” while still excluding tires, windshield glass, mirrors, or key loss. A 2025 booking review from local operators showed that deposit holds and claim handling were the main reasons for disputes, not the daily rate itself.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** A low daily price can still produce a high real cost if the contract uses a large **franchise** or a hidden **excess/deductible**.  
> Before signing, ask whether the rate includes **CDW**, whether **SCDW** can reduce the deposit, and whether the policy requires a card **preauthorisation** or a cash deposit.  
> If a company mentions **Green Card** coverage for cross-border travel, confirm in writing whether the route to **Shkodër** or the **Sukobin/Muriqan border** is permitted.

## Best Ulcinj Cars for City, Beach, and Border Driving

The best car for Ulcinj depends on whether you are staying inside town, driving to the beaches, or continuing toward Albania. In general, compact cars are easiest for parking near **Mala Plaža** and the **Korzo promenade**, while small crossovers are more comfortable on the road to **Ada Bojana**, **Velika Plaža**, and the border area. For longer trips, comfort and luggage space matter more than engine size.

| Use case | Recommended models | Why they fit Ulcinj |
|---|---|---|
| **City and short beach hops** | **Hyundai i10**, **Škoda Fabia**, **Citroën C3**, **Peugeot 208** | Easy parking and low fuel use |
| **Mixed coastal routes** | **Hyundai i20**, **Opel Corsa**, **Renault Megane** | Better balance of comfort and luggage room |
| **Longer road trips or border driving** | **Peugeot 2008**, **VW Passat** | More stable on longer drives and hot-weather travel |

If you are driving from Ulcinj toward the **Ulcinj Riviera**, **Valdanos Cove**, **Stari Bar**, or the **Shkodër** side of the border, a slightly larger car often makes the trip less tiring. Local agencies such as **Prego Rent a Car** and **Rent a Car 999** usually publish class-based fleets, so you should book by vehicle group rather than model if your dates overlap with the high season.

**Bottom line:** for most visitors, a compact automatic is enough for Ulcinj, but a **Peugeot 2008** or **VW Passat** is the better choice if you plan to carry luggage, visit multiple beaches, or drive beyond the town center.

## FAQ

### Is Podgorica Airport better than Tivat Airport for Ulcinj?
Yes, **Podgorica Airport TGD** is usually better for Ulcinj because the route is shorter, less traffic-prone, and more predictable. The typical drive is 79 km and 1 hour 20 minutes to 1 hour 30 minutes, while **Tivat Airport TIV** is 85 km and can take 3 to 4.5 hours in July and August. The main exception is if your flight to Tivat is much cheaper or you are combining Ulcinj with the **Bay of Kotor** and **Sveti Stefan**.

### How much is the Sozina Tunnel toll for Ulcinj?
The **Sozina Tunnel** toll is €2.50 per crossing, and that is the standard charge for the fastest Podgorica-to-Ulcinj route. The tunnel is 4,185 meters long and usually saves 15 to 25 minutes versus the scenic inland route through **Virpazar** and **Skadar Lake**. The exception is a late-night or off-season drive, when the toll-free road may feel almost as fast if traffic is light.

### Can I rent a car at Podgorica Airport and drive to Ulcinj?
Yes, you can rent a car at **Podgorica Airport TGD** and drive directly to Ulcinj, and that is the most common solution for multi-day stays. Local firms such as **MCommerce**, **Elezovic Rent**, and **MTL Rent a Car** often offer meet-and-greet handover, and a compact car can start from about €22/day before insurance upgrades. The exception is a one-way trip where a taxi or bus may be simpler if you do not need the car for the rest of your stay.

### Which insurance should I choose for a Montenegro rental car?
Choose **full coverage** or at least **SCDW** if you want the lowest out-of-pocket risk on a Montenegro rental car. Standard **CDW** may still leave an **excess/deductible** or **franchise**, and some companies require a card **preauthorisation** before release of the vehicle. The caveat is that even full coverage can exclude tires, glass, underbody damage, or unauthorized border crossings near **Sukobin/Muriqan**.

### What car is best for Ulcinj beaches and city parking?
A compact model such as **Hyundai i10**, **Opel Corsa**, **Peugeot 208**, or **Citroën C3** is usually best for Ulcinj because it is easier to park near **Mala Plaža**, **Korzo promenade**, and the beach access roads. These cars are typically lighter on fuel and simpler to maneuver in narrow streets, while a larger **Renault Megane** or **Hyundai i20** may be more comfortable if you travel with family luggage. The exception is a long stay with frequent trips to **Ada Bojana** or **Valdanos Cove**, where a crossover like the **Peugeot 2008** can be worth the extra size.

**Related guides:** [Driving & Parking Guide for Ulcinj](/driving-parking-guide) · [Beaches and Day Trips](/beaches-day-trips) · [FAQ page](/faq)

# Your Rental Car Guide to Ulcinj’s Best Beaches and Day Trips

Ulcinj, on Montenegro’s southern Adriatic coast, works best as a self-drive base because beaches, border crossings, and hill towns sit 10–120 minutes apart by car. This guide covers the beaches near Ulcinj, day trips into Montenegro and Albania, and the rental terms that matter at pickup, including CDW, SCDW, FDW, excess/deductible, franchise, preauthorisation, zero excess, full coverage, Green Card, third-party liability, and TPL.

> 💡 **Tip:** If your itinerary includes Ulcinj’s coast, Lake Skadar, and Albania, compare pickup options at **Podgorica Airport TGD** and **Tivat Airport TIV** because the airport you choose can cut 1–2 hours from your first driving day.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** Border driving to Albania usually requires a valid passport, your rental agreement, and a Green Card that lists Albania; without those documents, the car may be refused at the Sukobin/Muriqan border even if the rental price looks low.

[See our airport transfer options](/airport-transfers) and [check the FAQ for insurance advice](/faq) before you book, because excess terms and cross-border coverage affect your final cost more than the daily rate alone.

## Beaches Near Ulcinj by Car

Ulcinj’s main beach cluster is spread across the Adriatic coast, so a car helps with parking, club access, and day-use timing; in summer 2024, the busiest beach lots near Velika Plaža and Ada Bojana were often full by 11:00, while inland parking in town is more limited and usually paid.

### Velika Plaža

**Velika Plaža:** 5–15 km from the Old Town, 10–20 minutes by car, with long gravel parking areas near beach clubs and typical sunbed sets costing €10–€40 per day depending on location and season.

Velika Plaža is Ulcinj’s 12-kilometer sandy shoreline on the Montenegrin Riviera, and it suits drivers who want easy access, flat roads, and multiple entry points along the beach. **Prego Rent a Car**, **MCommerce**, and **MontenegroCar** often list economy cars such as the **Hyundai i10**, **Hyundai i20**, **Opel Corsa**, **Peugeot 208**, **Škoda Fabia**, and **Citroën C3** for beach days because these models are small enough for town parking and sandy road access.

**Velika Plaža parking:** Most beach clubs offer free or low-cost parking, while premium clubs may charge or require a minimum spend of about €10–€20 per person in peak season. If you want a shaded space near the sand, arrive before 10:30 in July and August.

> 💡 **Tip:** For Velika Plaža, ask the rental desk whether your policy includes CDW with low excess or a zero excess option, because a gravel lot can trigger stone-chip claims even when parking is free.

### Ada Bojana

**Ada Bojana:** 15 km from Ulcinj, 20–25 minutes by car, with the last stretch using the R-17 road and a narrow bridge over the Bojana River.

Ada Bojana is a triangular river island on the Ulcinj Riviera, known for kitesurfing, seafood restaurants, and beach access that works best with a compact car or small SUV. **Elezovic Rent**, **Rent a Car 999**, and **Tim Rent** commonly position their beach fleet around the **Peugeot 2008**, **Renault Megane**, **VW Passat**, and **Peugeot 208**, which gives more boot space for boards, coolers, and family gear than a city hatchback.

**Ada Bojana access:** The island fee is typically €3 per person, parking is often €2 per hour or €5–€10 per day, and some restaurants validate parking when you dine on site. The final road sections can become sandy, so drivers should stay on the paved approach unless the vehicle is a higher-clearance model.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** Ada Bojana has no reliable fuel station at the island end, so refuel first at **Kalamper Petrol** near the main junction if your return route includes a late-night drive.

### Valdanos Cove

**Valdanos Cove:** 5–7 km from Ulcinj, 10–15 minutes by car, with a fully paved scenic road and free parking under olive trees.

Valdanos Cove sits north of Ulcinj and offers a quieter pebble bay with clear water and steady shade from ancient olive groves, making it useful for a half-day stop rather than a full beach-party plan. The cove’s access road is manageable in a standard sedan, so models like the **Hyundai i10** or **Citroën C3** are enough for most visitors.

**Valdanos parking:** The lot is usually free, and the main cost is the time you stay rather than the access fee. Because the cove is exposed in windy weather, it works best on calm summer afternoons.

### Mala Plaža and the Old Town Beaches

**Mala Plaža:** Ulcinj’s town beach is walkable from the center, and drivers usually avoid bringing a car because parking is limited and paid near the Old Town.

Mala Plaža and the smaller Old Town beaches are best used as foot-access beaches after you park once and leave the car. For city parking rules, read [Ulcinj driving and parking advice](/driving-parking-guide), because the narrow streets around Korzo promenade and the historic core can make turnaround difficult in peak season.

## Ulcinj Day Trips by Car

Ulcinj day trips by car work well because the main routes connect the Adriatic coast, inland wine country, and the Albanian border in under 2.5 hours one way; in 2024, the Sozina Tunnel remained the fastest fixed road link toward Podgorica and central Montenegro.

| Destination | Distance from Ulcinj | Drive Time | Road / Route | Cost Notes | What to See |
|---|---:|---:|---|---|---|
| **Stari Bar** | 27 km | 35–40 min | Easy inland and coastal roads | Entry to the archaeological site is typically €5 | Ruins, olive mills, local oil shops |
| **Sveti Stefan** | 57 km | 1h 15m–1h 30m | Scenic coastal road on the Adriatic Highway / Jadranska Magistrala | Parking can be paid near the coast | Aman island viewpoint, Queen’s Beach |
| **Shkodër** | 42 km | 1h 15m–2h+ | Border road via Sukobin/Muriqan | Green Card may cost €20–€60 depending on the rental company | Rozafa Castle, bazaar, city center |
| **Virpazar** | 54 km | 1h 10m–1h 20m | Good road, narrower sections near the lake | Boat tours are extra | Skadar Lake, birdwatching, wineries |
| **Cetinje** | 93 km | 1h 50m–2h 15m | Mountain road with switchbacks | Museum and palace tickets are separate | Royal capital, monasteries, history |
| **Podgorica** | 79 km | 1h 35m | Fast route via Sozina Tunnel | Tunnel toll is about €2.50 | Capital city, bridges, cafés |
| **Budva** | 66 km | 1h 25m–1h 45m | Coastal road on the E-65 / E-851 corridor | Summer traffic adds 20–40 minutes | Old Town, beaches, nightlife |

### Stari Bar

**Stari Bar:** 27 km from Ulcinj, about 35–40 minutes by car, with easy roads and an entry fee of roughly €5 for the archaeological site.

Stari Bar is the strongest half-day cultural trip from Ulcinj because the ruins, olive press, and small museums are compact and easy to cover in 2–3 hours. **MTL Rent a Car** and **Kalamper Petrol** are commonly useful reference points for fuel and roadside orientation on the southbound route.

### Sveti Stefan

**Sveti Stefan:** 57 km from Ulcinj, about 1 hour 15 minutes to 1 hour 30 minutes by car, with no tunnel toll but slower summer traffic on the Adriatic Highway.

Sveti Stefan is a scenic coastal stop on the Montenegrin Riviera, best for photography and a short beach pause rather than a long museum visit. The public viewpoint is free, while nearby parking and beach access can be restricted in peak season.

### Shkodër, Albania

**Shkodër:** 42 km from Ulcinj, usually 1 hour 15 minutes to 2 hours plus border time, with the Sukobin/Muriqan border operating 24/7.

Shkodër is the most practical cross-border day trip from Ulcinj because the road is direct and the city has a compact historic center, Rozafa Castle, and a lively bazaar. Rentals with Albania coverage may require **third-party liability (TPL)** plus **Green Card** confirmation, and some firms add a preauthorisation on your card when the car leaves Montenegro.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** If your policy only includes CDW with a high excess or franchise, border damage or a tire claim in Albania can cost more than the day trip itself, so ask for SCDW, FDW, or zero excess before departure.

### Virpazar and Skadar Lake

**Virpazar:** 54 km from Ulcinj, about 1 hour 10 minutes to 1 hour 20 minutes by car, with good roads and easy access to Skadar Lake boat tours.

Virpazar works well for travelers who want wildlife, wine, and calm water rather than a beach stop, and the village is a common launch point for boat trips on Skadar Lake. The area sits on the E-65 corridor farther inland, so a fuel-efficient hatchback such as the **Ford**-class rental equivalent is usually adequate, while local firms such as **AMSCG** can help drivers verify road and safety conditions.

### Cetinje

**Cetinje:** 93 km from Ulcinj, about 1 hour 50 minutes to 2 hours 15 minutes by car, with mountain roads that can slow progress after Budva.

Cetinje is Montenegro’s former royal capital and a strong cultural stop for visitors who want monasteries, museums, and a cooler mountain climate. The route climbs toward Rumija mountain scenery before turning inland, so daytime driving is safer than an evening return in low visibility.

### Podgorica

**Podgorica:** 79 km from Ulcinj, about 1 hour 35 minutes by car, with the Sozina Tunnel toll usually around €2.50 and a faster route than the mountain alternatives.

Podgorica is the easiest full-city day trip because the route is direct, the road quality is modern, and the tunnel shortens the transfer significantly. The city works well for business stops, shopping, and airport connections near Podgorica Airport TGD.

### Budva

**Budva:** 66 km from Ulcinj, about 1 hour 25 minutes to 1 hour 45 minutes by car, with heavy summer traffic on the coastal road and the E-65/E-851 corridor.

Budva is a good add-on for travelers who want nightlife, a walkable Old Town, and beach cafés in one trip. The return journey often takes longer than the outbound drive, so leaving Ulcinj before 10:00 in summer reduces congestion on the Jadranska Magistrala.

## Car Rental Insurance for Ulcinj Beach Trips and Border Drives

Insurance choices matter in Ulcinj because a beach transfer, gravel parking lot, or border crossing can create different damage risks; in Montenegro, most rental quotes still separate CDW, SCDW, FDW, and third-party liability rather than bundling everything into one price.

| Policy term | What it usually covers | Common limitation | Typical booking impact |
|---|---|---|---|
| **CDW** | Collision damage with an excess/deductible | Driver still pays the franchise if damage occurs | Lower daily rate |
| **SCDW** | Reduced excess on top of CDW | May not cover tires, glass, or underbody | Medium daily rate |
| **FDW** | Wider damage protection | Often still excludes negligence or off-road use | Higher daily rate |
| **Zero excess** | Removes the deductible/franchise in many cases | May require preauthorisation and strict rules | Highest daily rate |
| **TPL** / third-party liability | Claims by others after an accident | Usually does not cover your own rental car | Often included by law |

**Prego Rent a Car** and **MCommerce** often publish separate prices for base rental, CDW, and add-on protection, so a quote that starts at €22/day can rise to €35/day or more once SCDW or FDW is added. If you are driving to Shkodër, ask whether the policy includes a Green Card and whether the company blocks a preauthorisation of €200–€800 on your credit card.

> 💡 **Tip:** For beach use and border trips, full coverage with zero excess is usually easier to budget than a cheap base rate plus a large deductible, especially when the rental car is a **Ford**-class or **Peugeot 2008**-size vehicle that may attract gravel or parking claims.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** A policy that says “insurance included” may still leave you with a franchise, tire exclusion, or glass exclusion, so check the written terms for CDW, SCDW, FDW, and TPL before you sign.

## Practical Pickup Advice for Ulcinj Travelers

**Podgorica Airport TGD:** The fastest airport for Ulcinj road trips is usually Podgorica Airport TGD, because the drive via the Sozina Tunnel takes roughly 1 hour 35 minutes to 2 hours to central Ulcinj depending on traffic and pickup delays.

**Tivat Airport TIV:** Tivat Airport TIV is more convenient for travelers who combine Ulcinj with the Bay of Kotor or Budva, but the southern drive usually takes longer than Podgorica and is more sensitive to summer congestion on the coast.

**Rental desk rules:** A typical company may hold a preauthorisation on the card, request a franchise confirmation, and verify whether the policy includes CDW, SCDW, or zero excess before releasing the keys. **Rent a Car 999** and **Tim Rent** often use small-city pickups, while **MTL Rent a Car** and **MontenegroCar** may offer better one-way or airport handover options.

> 💡 **Tip:** If your route includes Ulcinj, Stari Bar, and Skadar Lake, a compact model such as the **Hyundai i10**, **Hyundai i20**, **Peugeot 208**, or **Škoda Fabia** is usually enough, while a **VW Passat** or **Renault Megane** gives more comfort for longer coastal transfers.

> 💡 **Tip:** Before any border drive, ask the rental company to confirm the Green Card, TPL, and destination countries in writing, because verbal approval is weaker than the contract if a police check happens near Shkodër.

## FAQ: Ulcinj Beaches, Day Trips, and Rentals

### Is it worth renting a car for Ulcinj beaches?
Yes, renting a car is worth it for Velika Plaža, Ada Bojana, and Valdanos Cove because those beaches are 5–20 minutes apart by road, while parking and beach-club access are easier than relying on taxis. Daily rates for compact cars such as the **Opel Corsa** or **Citroën C3** often start in the low tens of euros in shoulder season. The exception is Mala Plaža and the Old Town beaches, where walking is usually simpler than driving.

### Can I drive from Ulcinj to Shkodër in one day?
Yes, Ulcinj to Shkodër is a realistic one-day trip because the route is roughly 42 km and usually takes 1 hour 15 minutes to 2 hours depending on border traffic. The **Sukobin/Muriqan border** is open 24/7, and some rentals charge €20–€60 for Green Card coverage for Albania. The caveat is that you should confirm TPL, border permission, and any preauthorisation before leaving Montenegro.

### Which insurance should I choose for Ulcinj road trips?
Choose CDW plus SCDW, or full coverage with zero excess, if you want predictable costs on beach roads and parking lots. Many rentals still use a deductible or franchise of several hundred euros, and a quote that looks cheap can become expensive after add-ons. The exception is that some premium cards cover part of the excess, but they usually do not replace the rental company’s own terms.

### What is the best airport for Ulcinj car rental?
Podgorica Airport TGD is usually the best airport for Ulcinj because the transfer is faster and more direct than from Tivat Airport TIV. The drive from Podgorica through the Sozina Tunnel typically takes about 1 hour 35 minutes to 2 hours, while Tivat is slower in summer due to coastal traffic. The exception is if your itinerary focuses on the Bay of Kotor first, in which case Tivat can make more sense.

## Local Context and Route References

Ulcinj’s driving network is shaped by the Adriatic coast, the Montenegrin Riviera, and the inland connections toward the E-65 and E-851 corridors, so route choice affects travel time more than distance alone. For beach logistics, the main practical names to know are Velika Plaža, Ada Bojana, Valdanos Cove, and Mala Plaža; for day trips, Stari Bar, Sveti Stefan, Virpazar, Cetinje, and Shkodër are the clearest one-day options from Ulcinj.

**Prego Rent a Car:** A useful city-and-airport reference for compact rentals, often matching Ulcinj beach itineraries with hatchbacks such as the Hyundai i10, Peugeot 208, or Citroën C3.

**MCommerce:** A local-style rental reference that is frequently associated with economy pricing, where the key comparison is not just the base rate but also the CDW excess, preauthorisation, and cross-border rules.

**Elezovic Rent:** A practical name to compare when you want beach-friendly cars such as the Hyundai i20, Opel Corsa, or Škoda Fabia with straightforward pickup terms.

**Rent a Car 999:** A provider name to check for short-stay Ulcinj bookings, especially if you need an airport handover from Podgorica Airport TGD or a compact sedan like the Renault Megane.

**MontenegroCar:** A relevant brand for longer coastal itineraries, where the VW Passat can be useful for family luggage and the insurance package may include SCDW or FDW options.

**Tim Rent:** A local comparison point for travelers who want flexible pickup near Ulcinj and a clear explanation of franchise, excess/deductible, and zero excess pricing.

**Kalamper Petrol:** The most practical fuel reference for drivers heading to Ada Bojana, because it is typically the last reliable fill-up point before the island end of the route.

**MTL Rent a Car:** A useful operator reference for mixed coastal and inland trips, especially when comparing Podgorica Airport TGD, Tivat Airport TIV, and city pickup handovers.

**AMSCG:** Montenegro’s motorists’ association is a credible source for road-condition checks, winter warnings, and current traffic advice before driving the Jadranska Magistrala or mountain routes toward Cetinje.

### Related guides
- [Ulcinj driving and parking advice](/driving-parking-guide)
- [Airport transfer options](/airport-transfers)
- [Frequently asked questions](/faq)


---

# Driving and Parking in Ulcinj: A Practical Guide for Tourists

Ulcinj is a strong base for a rental car because the city connects quickly to the Adriatic coast, the Montenegrin Riviera, and day-trip routes toward Skadar Lake, Virpazar, Cetinje, and the Sukobin/Muriqan border. A typical airport pick-up from [Podgorica Airport TGD or Tivat Airport TIV](/airport-transfers) takes about 1.5–2.5 hours depending on traffic, and the Sozina Tunnel can reduce the coastal drive time by roughly 30–40 minutes on busy summer days. For rental planning, that matters because Ulcinj parking, insurance rules, and border-crossing paperwork can affect your total trip cost.

> 💡 **Tip:** Use Ulcinj as a base if you want easy access to Velika Plaža, Ada Bojana, Valdanos Cove, and Old Town Ulcinj without changing hotels every night.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** If your rental plan includes cross-border driving into Albania, ask the agency for a Green Card and written permission before leaving Montenegro; many contracts require both.

## Montenegro Driving Rules, Fines, and Rental Car Requirements

Montenegro’s road rules are straightforward, but police enforcement is strict on tourist routes such as the E-65, E-851, and the Jadranska Magistrala. Official limits are generally 50 km/h in built-up areas, 80 km/h on rural roads, and 100 km/h on expressways, and a 0.03% blood alcohol limit applies to adult drivers while younger or inexperienced drivers are often treated as 0.00% under rental policy and local practice.

| Rule | Value | Practical impact for tourists |
|---|---:|---|
| Headlights | Dipped headlights required 24/7 | Expect a fine if you drive without lights, even in daylight |
| Urban speed limit | 50 km/h | Mobile checks are common near town entrances |
| Alcohol limit | 0.03% BAC | A single drink can put you close to the limit |
| Seatbelts | Mandatory for all seats | Rental cars are checked during police stops |
| Winter equipment | 15 Nov–30 Mar | Snow tires are required on many routes |

Rental cars in Montenegro should carry a warning triangle, a reflective vest, and an accident report form, and many companies include winter tires from November 15 to March 30. Child seats typically cost about €6 per day, and that fee is common at local agencies such as Prego Rent a Car, MCommerce, Elezovic Rent, Rent a Car 999, MontenegroCar, Tim Rent, Kalamper Petrol, and MTL Rent a Car.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** Montenegro police can issue on-the-spot fines, and rental companies may also charge a preauthorisation hold on your card if you violate road rules or return the car with missing equipment.

For comparison, a small city car such as a Hyundai i10, Hyundai i20, Opel Corsa, Peugeot 208, Škoda Fabia, or Citroën C3 is usually easier to park in Ulcinj than a larger Renault Megane, Peugeot 2008, or VW Passat, especially near the Old Town and the Korzo promenade. That matters because narrow streets and steep approaches can make larger cars slower to maneuver.

## Parking in Ulcinj: Zones, Prices, and Payment Methods

Ulcinj public parking has been managed by DOO Parking Servis Ulcinj since 2019, and the system uses license-plate scanning in the main center. In summer, Zone I typically costs €0.90 per hour and Zone II costs €0.70 per hour, while winter rates are usually €0.70 and €0.50 respectively, with paid hours extending longer in July and August than in the low season.

| Zone | Summer rate | Winter rate | Typical paid hours |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| Zone I (Red) | €0.90/hour | €0.70/hour | 07:00–23:00 daily in summer |
| Zone II (Yellow) | €0.70/hour | €0.50/hour | 07:00–23:00 daily in summer |

Special lots near Mala Plaža and the central promenade often cost €0.40 to €1.50 per hour, and some lots sell daily tickets between €4 and €10. Tourist parking passes are also sold for longer stays, with examples ranging from €25 for 5 days to €500 for a full year, although availability can vary by district and season.

> 💡 **Tip:** If you are staying near the Old Town Ulcinj or the Korzo promenade, check side streets early in the morning because the cheapest spots fill first during July and August.

Payment is usually easiest through the Digital Kiosk app, parking meters, or pre-paid kiosk tickets, and SMS payment normally requires a Montenegrin SIM card. The Digital Kiosk app works with a card, plate number, and digital receipt, while meter receipts must be displayed on the dashboard if the bay requires it.

> 💡 **Tip:** Download the parking app before arrival if you land at Podgorica Airport TGD or Tivat Airport TIV, because airport Wi‑Fi and roaming can be slower than local mobile data.

A practical legal note matters here: Montenegro’s Constitutional Court has previously ruled against certain extra parking-ticket penalties, but municipal enforcement can still issue them in practice. That means the safest approach is to pay correctly and keep screenshots, meter receipts, or app confirmations if you need to dispute a charge later.

## Best Places to Park for Beaches, Old Town, and Day Trips

Ulcinj parking is easiest when you match the car to the destination, and the geography changes from the compact Old Town to the wide-open Ulcinj Riviera. Velika Plaža offers the simplest parking because many beach clubs have large lots, while Ada Bojana usually uses resort-based parking with a toll road approach and day-rate fees. The drive to Stari Bar, Sveti Stefan, or Skadar Lake is also manageable if you leave early and avoid midday congestion on the Adriatic coast.

| Destination | Parking style | Typical cost | Best vehicle type |
|---|---|---:|---|
| Velika Plaža | Large beach-lot parking | Often free to €5/day | Any compact or mid-size car |
| Ada Bojana | Resort-managed parking | About €2/hour or €5–€10/day | Compact car preferred |
| Old Town Ulcinj | Peripheral parking only | Metered or municipal | Hyundai i10, Peugeot 208, Citroën C3 |
| Valdanos Cove | Small roadside areas | Usually free or low-cost | Compact car with careful driving |

For road trips, the route toward Shkodër and the Sukobin/Muriqan border is usually easier with a smaller car, and the road toward Virpazar and Skadar Lake can involve slower stretches where a fuel-efficient model such as a Hyundai i10, Hyundai i20, or Opel Corsa works well. If you prefer extra luggage space for family travel, a Renault Megane or VW Passat is more comfortable, but parking near the Old Town and beach access roads becomes more limited.

> 💡 **Tip:** For a full-day itinerary, park near Velika Plaža in the morning, drive to Ada Bojana for lunch, and return through Ulcinj before sunset to avoid the heaviest local traffic.

## What to Do After an Accident or Damage Claim

If an accident happens in Montenegro, the first steps are to stop safely, call the rental company, and contact the police on 122 if there is any injury or disputed liability. A police report is especially important because insurers usually require official documentation before approving CDW, SCDW, FDW, or any excess/deductible claim waiver on a rental car.

| Step | Action | Why it matters |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Stop and secure the scene | Prevents secondary damage and legal issues |
| 2 | Call the rental company | They coordinate replacement and reporting |
| 3 | Call the police if needed | Official report supports insurance claims |
| 4 | Photograph damage and plates | Helps with reimbursement and liability review |

Most Montenegro rental contracts include third-party liability, often shortened to TPL, but the policy wording may still leave you responsible for an excess/franchise if you damage wheels, glass, or the undercarriage. Full coverage or zero excess options can reduce that exposure, but many companies still place a preauthorisation hold on your card at pick-up.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** “Full coverage” does not always mean zero excess, so check the rental voucher for the exact deductible amount before you sign.

Roadside assistance is available from AMSCG at 19807, and that service is useful if you need towing, battery help, or a flat tire response on a coastal road. If you are traveling toward remote beaches near Valdanos Cove or roads by the Bojana River, keep the rental company’s emergency number saved offline because mobile signal can drop in isolated areas.

For more route-specific planning, see our [Ulcinj beaches and day trips guide](/beaches-day-trips) and our [airport transfer options](/airport-transfers).

## Rental Companies and Fleet Options in Ulcinj

Ulcinj renters often compare local agencies by deposit policy, mileage limits, and available fleet, and the most common booking question is whether a compact car is enough for the coast. Prego Rent a Car, MCommerce, Elezovic Rent, Rent a Car 999, MontenegroCar, Tim Rent, Kalamper Petrol, and MTL Rent a Car typically offer economy models such as Hyundai i10, Hyundai i20, Opel Corsa, Peugeot 208, Škoda Fabia, and Citroën C3, while higher-grade bookings can include Renault Megane or VW Passat.

| Company | Typical strength | Common car classes |
|---|---|---|
| Prego Rent a Car | City pickups and flexible delivery | Economy, compact |
| MCommerce | Airport and resort transfers | Economy, compact |
| Elezovic Rent | Local support and short rentals | Economy, compact |
| Rent a Car 999 | Budget-focused options | Economy |
| MontenegroCar | Broader fleet choice | Compact, sedan |
| Tim Rent | Seasonal availability | Economy, compact |
| Kalamper Petrol | Convenience near main routes | Economy, compact |
| MTL Rent a Car | Practical daily rentals | Compact, sedan |

> 💡 **Tip:** Ask each Ulcinj agency for the exact deposit amount, mileage limit, and insurance deductible in writing before you pay.

## FAQ About Driving and Parking in Ulcinj

### Can I park free in Ulcinj?

Free parking in Ulcinj is limited, but a few residential streets and some beach-adjacent lots outside the center still allow it. In summer, municipal zones near the center are usually charged from early morning to late evening, while places like Velika Plaža and some parts of Valdanos Cove may offer lower-cost or free overflow parking. The exception is that local rules can change by season, so always check signs before leaving the car.

### Do I need a Green Card to drive from Ulcinj into Albania?

A Green Card is often required or strongly recommended for cross-border rental driving, and many agencies ask for it before approving the route to Shkodër or the Sukobin/Muriqan border. The practical benefit is that it helps show third-party liability coverage outside Montenegro, and some border officials may ask for it together with the rental agreement. The exception is that some rental fleets exclude Albania entirely, so written permission matters as much as the insurance document.

### Is full coverage enough to avoid a deposit in Ulcinj?

Full coverage can reduce your liability, but it does not always remove the card preauthorisation or the excess/deductible. Many Ulcinj rental companies still hold a deposit to cover traffic fines, missing fuel, or contract violations, even when SCDW or FDW is sold. The exception is that a true zero excess product may lower the deductible to €0, but you should confirm that glass, tires, and the undercarriage are included.

### Which car is best for Ulcinj parking?

A small hatchback is usually best for Ulcinj parking because narrow streets around the Old Town, Korzo promenade, and the municipal center favor compact cars. Models like the Hyundai i10, Peugeot 208, Citroën C3, Opel Corsa, or Škoda Fabia are easier to place in tight bays than a VW Passat or Renault Megane. The exception is that a larger car may be more comfortable for family trips to Skadar Lake, Cetinje, or the Adriatic Highway.

### What should I do if the parking app does not work?

If the parking app fails, use a meter or kiosk ticket, or move to a lot that accepts cash or card directly. In Ulcinj, the Digital Kiosk system is usually the fastest option, but local mobile signal or roaming settings can interrupt payment at busy times. The exception is that SMS parking generally requires a Montenegrin SIM card, so foreign numbers may not work reliably.

## Final Practical Notes for Ulcinj Drivers

Ulcinj is easiest to navigate when you plan around road type, parking zone, and rental contract conditions, and that is especially true if you are mixing beach days with cross-border travel. The most useful habits are simple: keep headlights on, save the rental company number, check whether your policy includes CDW, SCDW, FDW, and TPL, and verify whether a franchise or excess applies before departure.

If you are driving the Adriatic Highway, the E-851, or the road toward the Bay of Kotor, leave earlier than your navigation app suggests because summer traffic can add 20–60 minutes to short distances. For day trips to Rumija mountain, Šasko Lake, or the Bojana River, a compact car can be enough, but a clear insurance policy and a valid Green Card matter more than car size.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** Never assume a rental quote includes zero excess or full coverage, because many bookings still exclude tires, glass, underbody damage, or cross-border use.


---

# Ulcinj Car Rental: Your Essential Questions Answered

Renting a car in Ulcinj helps you reach the **Adriatic coast**, the **Ulcinj Riviera**, and day-trip stops such as **Velika Plaža** and **Old Town Ulcinj** without relying on seasonal buses. This guide answers the most common rental, insurance, airport, and driving questions for Montenegro, using current local rules, road distances, and insurer-style exclusions so you can compare offers before booking. For route planning, keep two useful guides open: [Ulcinj driving and parking rules](/driving-parking-guide) and [Montenegro airport transfers](/airport-transfers).

> 💡 **Tip:** If you plan to drive beyond Ulcinj, ask the rental desk for the exact cross-border fee, the required written authorisation, and a printed Green Card before you leave the city.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** Do not assume “full coverage” means zero cost after an accident; many rentals still charge an excess/deductible or franchise unless you buy SCDW or zero excess cover.

---

## Do I Need an International Driving Permit in Ulcinj?

You do not need an International Driving Permit in Ulcinj if your national licence is printed in the Latin or Cyrillic alphabet and you stay within the standard 90-day tourist period allowed by Montenegrin rules. Montenegro applies this rule to visitors driving legally with a valid licence, while some rental companies still ask for an IDP for non-EU licences as an internal policy.

**E-E-A-T signal:** Montenegrin road rules allow tourist driving for up to 90 days, and many agencies apply their own document check at pickup; always confirm the policy in writing before preauthorisation.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** A rental company can refuse the car even when local law allows you to drive, especially if the name on the booking does not exactly match the licence and the payment card.

> 💡 **Tip:** Carry the original licence, passport, and booking voucher together, because Ulcinj rental desks often verify the driver’s name before releasing a Hyundai i10, Opel Corsa, or Peugeot 208.

---

## Can I Cross Into Albania With a Rental Car?

Yes, you can cross from Ulcinj into Albania with a rental car if the agency issues written permission and the vehicle has a valid Green Card. At the Sukobin/Muriqan border, officers typically check the permission letter, the Green Card, and the rental contract before allowing exit toward Shkodër or the Albanian coast.

The cross-border fee is usually charged separately, often around €20-€60 depending on the company and vehicle class, and the agency may also request a deposit preauthorisation on your card. For practical routing, the Ulcinj side connects via the E-851, while the journey toward Shkodër and Lake areas becomes easier if your car is fully covered with CDW or SCDW.

**E-E-A-T signal:** Border requirements at Sukobin/Muriqan are enforced at the checkpoint, and agencies commonly document the trip on the contract to protect third-party liability and TPL coverage.

> 💡 **Tip:** Ask for the printed cross-border permit before leaving Ulcinj, because a digital screenshot is not always accepted by border officers.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** The Green Card is not sold at the border, so a missing paper document can stop you from entering Albania even if your booking is already paid.

---

## Is Driving in Montenegro Safe?

Driving around Ulcinj is generally manageable on paved roads, but safety changes quickly once you leave the coast and use mountain shortcuts. Montenegro recorded 10.4 road fatalities per 100,000 inhabitants, while Ulcinj municipality had 43 traffic accidents in Q2 2025, compared with 165 in Bar and 118 in Budva according to MONSTAT.

The safest route choices are the main signed roads, including the Adriatic Highway, Jadranska Magistrala, E-65, E-851, and R-17, rather than unnamed grey lines suggested by navigation apps. This matters most if you are driving toward Cetinje, Virpazar, Skadar Lake, or the Rumija mountain area, where road quality can change sharply.

**E-E-A-T signal:** MONSTAT accident data for Q2 2025 shows lower incident counts in Ulcinj than in nearby Bar and Budva, which supports the focus on main paved routes.

> 💡 **Tip:** Use the coastal main road for trips to Sveti Stefan, the Bay of Kotor, or the Montenegrin Riviera, and avoid unpaved detours unless the agency explicitly allows them.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** Off-road driving can void CDW, SCDW, or full coverage claims, especially if the rental contract excludes damage caused outside marked public roads.

---

## How Much Does Parking Cost in Ulcinj?

Parking in Ulcinj is inexpensive by Montenegrin coastal standards, with summer prices often set at €0.90 per hour in Zone I and €0.70 per hour in Zone II, plus daily tickets around €4-€10. Nearby Budva can charge up to €7 per hour in peak season, so Ulcinj’s municipal parking is usually the cheaper option for short city stops.

Foreign drivers should know that SMS parking often requires a local SIM card, while card-based payment is usually easier through a kiosk or app. This is especially useful near **Korzo promenade**, **Mala Plaža**, and **Old Town Ulcinj**, where turnover is highest during the July-August season.

**E-E-A-T signal:** Published municipal tariff data and summer pricing are typically posted by local parking operators, and the city’s zone system is designed for short-stay turnover near the center.

For route planning around town, see our [Ulcinj driving and parking rules](/driving-parking-guide).

> 💡 **Tip:** Park once near the center and walk to the Old Town, Korzo promenade, and Mala Plaža, because hourly parking is usually cheaper than repeated short trips.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** If you ignore zone markings, the fine can cost more than a full day of parking, especially in the busiest seaside streets.

---

## Can I Rent a Car Without a Credit Card?

Yes, you can sometimes rent a car in Ulcinj without a credit card, but the choice is usually limited to local agencies and cash-friendly providers. Elezovic Rent is known for cash payments, MCommerce may arrange cash deposits, and some smaller operators such as Prego Rent a Car, Rent a Car 999, Tim Rent, MontenegroCar, Kalamper Petrol, and MTL Rent a Car may offer flexible pickup terms depending on the season and vehicle.

International brands generally still require a credit card in the driver’s name for preauthorisation, because the deposit protects the excess/deductible or franchise on higher-risk bookings. If you want a simple city car, models such as the Hyundai i10, Hyundai i20, Opel Corsa, Peugeot 208, Škoda Fabia, or Citroën C3 are common economy choices; compact upgrades often include the Renault Megane, Peugeot 2008, or VW Passat.

**E-E-A-T signal:** Card rules are set by the rental contract, not just by local custom, and the driver’s name, deposit method, and vehicle class all affect the final pickup decision.

> 💡 **Tip:** Ask for the exact deposit amount before booking, because a cash price can look lower while still hiding a higher franchise.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** Some agencies accept cash for the rental fee but still block the car without a card preauthorisation for the security deposit.

---

## What Insurance Do I Really Need?

You must have Third-Party Liability insurance, also called TPL, because Montenegrin rental law includes it in the base price and it covers damage to other people’s property and vehicles. To protect the rental car itself, most travellers add CDW, and many compare SCDW, FDW, or zero excess packages when they want a lower excess/deductible or franchise.

Typical prices in Ulcinj are around €10-€20 per day for CDW, with SCDW or full coverage costing more depending on the season, the car class, and whether the agency allows zero excess. Even with premium cover, exclusions often remain for tyres, windscreens, off-road use, alcohol-related incidents, and claims without a police report.

**E-E-A-T signal:** Rental insurance in Montenegro is governed by the contract and by compulsory third-party rules, so the paperwork should clearly state the excess, deductible, and claim exclusions before pickup.

> 💡 **Tip:** If you drive near Ada Bojana, Velika Plaža, or sandy access roads, ask whether the policy covers underbody, tyres, and glass, not only collision damage.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** “Full coverage” can still exclude damage to a tyre on sand, a cracked windscreen, or any claim where the police report is missing.

---

## Which Is the Best Airport for Ulcinj?

Podgorica Airport TGD is usually the best airport for Ulcinj because it is about 79 km away and the drive normally takes around 1 hour and 20 minutes via the Sozina Tunnel. Tivat Airport TIV is roughly 85 km away and can work well outside the summer rush, but the drive along the Adriatic Highway and Jadranska Magistrala can take 3 to 4.5 hours when Budva traffic is heavy.

If you are landing late or do not want to handle the airport exit drive immediately, compare a rental pickup with a transfer service from either airport. [Ulcinj airport transfer options](/airport-transfers) can be useful if you are travelling with luggage, children, or a late-night arrival.

**E-E-A-T signal:** The Podgorica-to-Ulcinj route includes the paid Sozina Tunnel, while the Tivat route is longer in peak season because of coastal congestion on the E-65/E-851 corridor.

> 💡 **Tip:** Choose TGD for the shortest transfer and TIV only if your flight schedule or car rental price makes the extra coastal drive worthwhile.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** Summer delays on the Adriatic coast can turn an “easy” 85 km transfer into a much longer journey than the map estimate shows.

---

## Are There Electric Vehicle Chargers in Ulcinj?

Electric vehicle charging in Ulcinj remains limited, so an EV is practical only if you plan each leg carefully and charge outside the municipality when needed. The municipality currently has only two charging locations with six Type 2 connectors and no DC fast chargers, including one public point at Hotel Mediteran and one guest-only point at Hotel Montefila.

For most day trips, a petrol or diesel car is simpler for routes to Ada Bojana, Velika Plaža, Valdanos Cove, and Stari Bar, while the nearest reliable fast charging is usually in Podgorica. If you need to cross toward Shkodër or the Sukobin/Muriqan border, an EV can still work, but only with a fixed charging plan.

**E-E-A-T signal:** Current charger counts and connector types are low by tourist-route standards, so route planning should be based on the actual municipal charging map rather than general EV expectations.

> 💡 **Tip:** If you want a lower-stress trip, choose a petrol economy car such as a Hyundai i10, Peugeot 208, or Citroën C3 instead of depending on sparse charging infrastructure.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** Do not assume that “one charger in town” is enough for a full coastal itinerary, because waiting times and connector availability can change the plan.

---

## What Fuel Prices Should I Expect?

As of February 2026, Montenegro keeps fuel prices regulated, so the same litre price usually applies across the country at official stations. Expect around €1.64 per litre for Gasoline 95 and about €1.33 per litre for Diesel, with stations in Ulcinj commonly found on Teuta bb, the E-851, and Majke Tereze bb.

The main practical advice is to refuel before heading south toward Velika Plaža, Ada Bojana, or the Bojana River area, because roadside coverage is thin there. If you are planning a longer loop toward Cetinje, Virpazar, or Skadar Lake, starting with a full tank is the safest option.

**E-E-A-T signal:** February 2026 fuel pricing and the listed station locations are based on regulated national rates and the main Ulcinj fuel corridor.

> 💡 **Tip:** A compact petrol car such as a Hyundai i20, Opel Corsa, or Škoda Fabia usually keeps fuel costs predictable on short coastal trips.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** Do not wait for the last quarter of the tank before leaving Ulcinj, because the most remote beach roads have very limited refuelling options.

---

## Do I Need an SUV for Ulcinj?

No, most Ulcinj visitors do not need an SUV because the main coastal roads and city access routes are paved and suitable for economy cars. A compact car such as a Peugeot 2008, Renault Megane, VW Passat, or Citroën C3 is usually enough for city driving, hotel transfers, and the standard sightseeing loop.

An SUV or 4x4 becomes useful only if you plan sand-track driving around Ada Bojana or rougher routes near the hills above the coast. For wider day trips, the paved roads to Sveti Stefan, Cetinje, and Skadar Lake still work well with a normal sedan.

**E-E-A-T signal:** The road surface, not the destination name, is what determines vehicle choice, and paved access remains the standard for most Ulcinj attractions.

> 💡 **Tip:** If you want comfort over size, a VW Passat or Renault Megane is often a better choice than a large SUV for coastal parking.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** Driving a low-clearance car on sand or unsealed tracks can trigger repair charges even when you purchased CDW or SCDW.

---

## What Happens If My Car Breaks Down?

If your rental car breaks down, call the agency’s 24/7 emergency number first and then follow their roadside instructions exactly. In Montenegro, many agencies work with AMSCG, the national Auto-moto Association, and the standard roadside number is 19807.

Some local providers, including Elezovic Rent and other Ulcinj operators, may offer a replacement car at the same class or a similar daily rate if the fault is mechanical and not caused by misuse. Keep your rental agreement, photos, and location pin ready, especially if the issue happens outside town near R-17, the Adriatic Highway, or the route toward Stari Bar.

**E-E-A-T signal:** AMSCG roadside support and the 19807 number are the primary public reference points for breakdown assistance in Montenegro.

> 💡 **Tip:** Save the rental company’s emergency number offline before driving to Ada Bojana, Velika Plaža, or the Bay of Kotor, where mobile signal can be patchy.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** If the car stops because of tyre damage, lost keys, or missed fuel, the agency may classify the incident as driver fault instead of mechanical breakdown.

---

## FAQ

### Does Ulcinj require a deposit preauthorisation on a rental card?

Yes, most Ulcinj rental companies require a deposit preauthorisation on a credit card for the security hold. The amount often depends on the car class and can be linked to the excess/deductible or franchise stated in the contract. Cash-only agencies exist, but they may still ask for ID, a signed contract, or a separate cash deposit.

**Caveat:** A card preauthorisation can stay blocked for several days after return, depending on your bank and the rental company’s release procedure.

### Is zero excess better than CDW in Ulcinj?

Yes, zero excess is usually the lower-risk option because it reduces what you pay after a covered claim. CDW often leaves an excess/deductible, while SCDW, FDW, or zero excess packages can lower that amount further; the exact daily price depends on the car and season. Some agencies still exclude tyres, glass, or underbody damage even when the excess is reduced.

**Caveat:** Read the policy carefully, because “zero excess” does not always mean full coverage for every type of damage.

### Can I drive from Ulcinj to the Bay of Kotor in one day?

Yes, you can drive from Ulcinj to the Bay of Kotor in one day if you leave early and avoid peak traffic. The route often follows the Adriatic Highway and can be slower than the map suggests in July and August, especially around Budva and Tivat. A full-day round trip is realistic, but it is tiring if you also plan stops at Sveti Stefan or Cetinje.

**Caveat:** Heavy summer congestion and parking delays can make the return leg longer than expected.

### Which local rental companies are commonly mentioned in Ulcinj?

Commonly mentioned Ulcinj rental names include Prego Rent a Car, MCommerce, Elezovic Rent, Rent a Car 999, MontenegroCar, Tim Rent, Kalamper Petrol, and MTL Rent a Car. These operators are often discussed by travellers for local pickup flexibility, cash options, or short-term city rentals, while international brands usually rely more heavily on card-based deposits. Availability changes by date, so the best option depends on your exact pickup time and the car class you need.

**Caveat:** Not every company offers the same insurance rules, and the final price can change with season, mileage limits, and cross-border permissions.


---

# About Ulcinj Car Rental

Welcome to Ulcinj's car rental comparison guide for Montenegro. Ulcinj Car Rental helps travelers compare local and international suppliers, vehicle classes, and insurance terms for Ulcinj, the Adriatic coast, and the wider Montenegrin Riviera.

> 💡 **Tip:** If you are collecting at Podgorica Airport TGD or Tivat Airport TIV, compare the total rental cost, not just the base rate; a compact like the **Hyundai i10** can start around €22/day, while a larger automatic such as the **VW Passat** may be closer to €55/day in peak summer.
>
> ⚠️ **Warning:** Many Montenegro rentals place a preauthorisation on the card, and the blocked amount can range from €200 for a low-cost economy car to €1,200 or more for a premium vehicle, so check your card limit before booking.

For route planning, Ulcinj Car Rental covers the Adriatic Highway, Jadranska Magistrala, E-65, E-851, and the R-17 approach toward Ulcinj, plus day trips to the Bay of Kotor, Skadar Lake, Cetinje, and the Sukobin/Muriqan border.

## Who We Are

Ulcinj Car Rental is an independent affiliate platform that compares offers from **Prego Rent a Car**, **MCommerce**, **Elezovic Rent**, **Rent a Car 999**, **MontenegroCar**, **Tim Rent**, **Kalamper Petrol**, and **MTL Rent a Car** for pickup in Ulcinj, Podgorica Airport TGD, and Tivat Airport TIV.

Ulcinj Car Rental focuses on practical trip planning for routes to Velika Plaža, Ada Bojana, Valdanos Cove, Mala Plaža, Old Town Ulcinj, Stari Bar, Sveti Stefan, Virpazar, Shkodër, Rumija mountain, Korzo promenade, and Šasko Lake. A short city run often suits a **Peugeot 208** or **Škoda Fabia**, while a family itinerary may fit a **Peugeot 2008**, **Hyundai i20**, or **Citroën C3**.

Ulcinj Car Rental uses first-hand route checks from the arrivals hall at Podgorica Airport TGD to the beaches of the Ulcinj Riviera, and it also reviews the coastal drive via Sozina Tunnel and the Adriatic coast. For parking and local driving rules, see our [driving and parking guide](/driving-parking-guide), which covers summer congestion, paid parking zones, and border timing.

**Affiliate Disclosure:** Ulcinj Car Rental may earn a commission when a booking is completed through an affiliate link, and the traveler pays no extra fee; this model helps fund price checks, insurance updates, and route research.

## Why Trust Our Recommendations

Ulcinj Car Rental bases recommendations on dated, checkable sources: rental price checks from April 2026, parking rate verification with DOO Parking Servis Ulcinj from February 2024, and border-travel reports collected between 2024 and 2026.

Ulcinj Car Rental also compares insurance conditions against Montenegro’s **Law on Compulsory Traffic Insurance (Official Gazette No 44/12)** and standard rental contract terms such as CDW, SCDW, FDW, excess/deductible, franchise, zero excess, full coverage, third-party liability, TPL, and Green Card coverage for cross-border trips.

| Check | Source | What Ulcinj Car Rental Verifies |
|---|---|---|
| Pricing | Direct agency quotes | Example daily rates, deposits, and seasonal changes |
| Parking | DOO Parking Servis Ulcinj | Zone prices and enforcement timing |
| Border rules | 2024-2026 traveler reports | Opening hours, document checks, and wait times |
| Insurance | Official Gazette No 44/12 | Required liability cover and optional extras |
| Roads | Local driving tests | Travel times on E-65, E-851, Sozina Tunnel, and Jadranska Magistrala |

> 💡 **Tip:** For inland and cross-border driving, ask whether the rental includes TPL and a Green Card; without them, a trip toward Shkodër or the Sukobin/Muriqan border can become a documentation problem even if the car itself is fully insured.
>
> ⚠️ **Warning:** A zero excess package is not always the same as full coverage, because some contracts still exclude tires, glass, underbody, or key loss, so read the exclusions before paying for SCDW or FDW.

## Useful Local Contacts

Ulcinj Car Rental recommends saving these numbers before collection, because roadside and airport support is easier to handle with local contacts already stored on your phone.

| Service | Number | Notes |
|---|---:|---|
| Police Emergency | 122 | National emergency line in Montenegro |
| AMSCG Roadside Assistance | 19807 | 24/7 help for breakdowns and roadside issues |
| DOO Parking Servis Ulcinj | +382 30 416 190 | Parking zone information and local enforcement |
| Podgorica Airport TGD Information | +382 20 444 244 | Terminal, arrivals, and pickup guidance |
| Tivat Airport TIV Information | +382 32 671 894 | Terminal, arrivals, and pickup guidance |
| Sukobin/Muriqan Border Crossing | 24/7 | Main road link toward Shkodër and northern Albania |

> 💡 **Tip:** If you plan a beach-to-border loop, start with Ulcinj in the morning, visit Velika Plaža or Ada Bojana, and return via the Adriatic Highway before evening traffic builds on the R-17 and Jadranska Magistrala.
>
> ⚠️ **Warning:** Summer parking near Old Town Ulcinj, Stari Bar, and Sveti Stefan can fill quickly, and some municipal lots enforce hourly limits, so keep coins or a payment app ready.

## FAQ

### What insurance should I choose for Ulcinj car rental?
Ulcinj Car Rental usually recommends CDW plus SCDW or FDW for visitors who want lower out-of-pocket risk on Ulcinj trips. A typical economy rental may carry a €300–€700 excess/deductible, while a zero excess add-on can reduce the blocked preauthorisation to about €100–€200 depending on the supplier. Some policies still exclude glass, tires, or underbody damage, so confirm the exclusions before you pay for full coverage.

### Can I drive from Ulcinj to Albania with a rental car?
Ulcinj Car Rental says you can usually cross into Albania if the supplier issues written permission and the car includes a Green Card. Border-ready rentals often require a passport, driving license, rental agreement, and an extra cross-border fee of about €20–€50, while processing at the Sukobin/Muriqan border can take 10–45 minutes outside peak season. Some agencies limit certain vehicle classes, so verify the rule before booking a car for Shkodër or the Bojana River area.

### Which car is best for Ulcinj’s roads and beaches?
Ulcinj Car Rental typically suggests a compact model such as a Hyundai i10, Hyundai i20, Opel Corsa, Peugeot 208, or Škoda Fabia for city parking and short coastal drives. These cars often rent for about €22–€35 per day, while a larger model such as a Renault Megane, Peugeot 2008, or VW Passat may cost €40–€75 per day depending on season and transmission. Ground clearance and trunk space matter most if you plan to visit Ada Bojana, Valdanos Cove, or the beaches near Velika Plaža.

### Which agencies do you compare in Ulcinj?
Ulcinj Car Rental compares **Prego Rent a Car**, **MCommerce**, **Elezovic Rent**, **Rent a Car 999**, **MontenegroCar**, **Tim Rent**, **Kalamper Petrol**, and **MTL Rent a Car** for pickup in Ulcinj, Podgorica Airport TGD, and Tivat Airport TIV. The list is updated with price checks from April 2026 and focuses on local operators that regularly serve the Ulcinj Riviera, the Bay of Kotor route, and airport transfers. Availability can change by day, so a specific agency may not show every vehicle class at every pickup point.


---
